title: Get the death verified and the MCCD issued
description: Have the death verified by a healthcare professional; the medical examiner scrutinises the cause of death and issues the Medical Certificate of Cause of Death (MCCD). If the death is referred to the coroner, await the interim certificate; an inquest can delay registration.

title: Tell close family, friends and any employer
description: Tell close family and friends. If the person was working, tell the employer.

title: Find the will, codicils and funeral wishes
description: Locate the will, any codicils and any recorded funeral wishes. Check for a prepaid funeral plan.

title: Secure the home, belongings and pets; insure the empty property
description: Secure the property and belongings and care for any pets. Make sure the empty home is insured and tell the insurer it is unoccupied.

title: Arrange the funeral
description: Arrange the funeral through a director or directly, following any recorded wishes or prepaid plan. Consider the DWP Funeral Expenses Payment only if a person on a qualifying benefit is paying for the funeral.

title: Register the death within 5 days and order certified copies
description: Register at the register office, ideally in the area where the death occurred. Order several certified copies of the death certificate; many organisations require an original.

title: Give the certificate for burial or cremation to the funeral director
description: Pass the certificate for burial or cremation (the green form) issued at registration to the funeral director.

title: Use Tell Us Once within 28 days
description: Use the Tell Us Once service within 28 days of receiving the reference number at registration. It notifies DWP, HMRC, the Passport Office, DVLA, the council, public-sector pension schemes and Veterans UK in one step.

title: Notify banks, building societies, NS&I and Premium Bonds
description: Tell every bank and building society, and NS&I including any Premium Bonds. A free bulk service can help: the Death Notification Service (UK Finance) covers most banks and building societies; Life Ledger and Settld cover a wider set of companies.

title: Notify pension providers, including any armed forces service pension
description: Tell private and workplace pension providers. If the person held an armed forces service pension, notify the Armed Forces Pension Scheme; payments stop and arrears or a short continuation may apply. Check service charities (for example the relevant benevolent fund, the Royal British Legion and SSAFA) for bereavement support and possible help with funeral costs.

title: Notify insurers
description: Tell life, home, car, health and pet insurers. Keep the empty-home policy live and compliant with any unoccupancy conditions.

title: Notify the mortgage lender, or the landlord or housing association
description: Tell the mortgage lender if the person owned the home, or the landlord or housing association if they rented.

title: Notify utility suppliers
description: Tell the electricity, gas and water suppliers. Take meter readings and keep supplies as needed for an empty property.

title: Notify telecoms providers
description: Tell the landline, broadband and mobile phone providers.

title: Notify TV Licensing and streaming or subscription services
description: Tell TV Licensing and cancel or transfer streaming and other subscription services.

title: Notify health and care providers
description: Tell the GP, dentist and optician, cancel any hospital appointments, and tell any care agency involved.

title: Notify the employer or former employer and professional bodies
description: Tell the employer or former employer (there may be pension or death-in-service benefits) and any professional bodies.

title: Notify clubs, societies and memberships
description: Tell clubs, societies and membership organisations, including any local library, so memberships are closed or transferred.

title: Deal with digital accounts
description: Memorialise or close social media accounts, and deal with email, cloud and photo accounts, domains and digital wallets according to each provider's bereavement process.

title: Cancel subscriptions, memberships and recurring payments
description: Cancel recurring direct debits, standing orders, subscriptions and memberships. Check bank statements for regular payments to catch anything not already known.

title: Redirect post and stop marketing mail
description: Set up Royal Mail redirection for the person's post and register with the Bereavement Register to stop marketing mail.

title: Deal with any vehicle
description: Sort the vehicle: insurance, SORN or sale or transfer, and update the DVLA registered keeper details.

title: Claim the Council Tax Class F exemption
description: Claim the Class F council tax exemption on the empty property while it is awaiting probate.

title: Trace forgotten assets via the free official services
description: Search for anything forgotten using the free official services: My Lost Account (banks, building societies and NS&I), the Pension Tracing Service (gov.uk) and the Unclaimed Assets Register (ABI). Also sweep post, email, statements and tax records, and check for a safe deposit box, loyalty schemes and digital wallets. Never pay a reclaim firm; these searches are free.

title: Confirm the executors named in the will
description: Confirm who the will names as executors and that they are willing and able to act.

title: Open an executors' bank account
description: Open a dedicated executors' account so estate money is kept separate and every movement is traceable.

title: Value the estate
description: Value every asset and liability at the date of death: request date-of-death balances from each institution, obtain a RICS valuation for any property, and record whether each figure is an estimate or confirmed.

title: Assess inheritance tax and determine the reporting route
description: Assess the IHT position from the valuations and determine whether the estate is excepted or a full IHT400 is required. Any claim to the residence nil-rate band or its transfer forces a full IHT400 even if no tax is payable.

title: Complete the IHT400 and required schedules; arrange payment
description: Complete the IHT400 and the schedules the estate's contents require, and arrange payment of any tax due (Direct Payment Scheme from the deceased's accounts, or instalments where available).

title: Pay any inheritance tax due by the statutory deadline
description: Pay any IHT due by the end of the sixth month after the month of death; interest runs on late payment.

title: Submit the IHT400 and wait for the HMRC code
description: Submit the IHT400 to HMRC and wait for the code needed by the probate service (roughly 20 working days) before applying for probate.

title: Apply for probate and receive the grant
description: Apply for probate (online where possible), pay the application fee, and receive the grant of probate.

title: Place the Section 27 creditor notice
description: Place a deceased-estates notice in The Gazette and a local newspaper. The claim window is two months from placement and protects the executors from unknown creditor claims before distribution.

title: Collect the assets
description: Collect in the assets: close accounts, encash or transfer holdings, and sell or transfer any property as the will directs.

title: Settle debts and creditors
description: Settle all verified debts and creditor claims in the correct priority order before distributing anything.

title: Finalise administration-period income tax and capital gains tax
description: Finalise income tax to the date of death and for the administration period, and any CGT on disposals above probate value. A residential-property gain must be reported and paid within 60 days of completion. Check whether the informal route applies or the estate is complex.

title: Prepare estate accounts and make interim distributions
description: Prepare estate accounts showing capital, administration, income and distribution, and make any interim distributions only once the creditor-notice window has closed and creditors are settled or provisioned.

title: Wait out the claims window, then make the final distribution
description: Wait out the six-month post-grant claims window under the Inheritance (Provision for Family and Dependants) Act before the final distribution, then distribute the residue and obtain receipts or approvals from the residuary beneficiaries.

title: Apply for the IHT30 clearance certificate and retain records
description: Apply to HMRC for the IHT30 clearance certificate once the IHT position is final, and retain the estate records for around 12 years for HMRC and executor protection.

title: Arrange bereavement support
description: Support is available throughout: Cruse Bereavement Support, the Age UK advice line, Marie Curie, Sue Ryder and the GP.

title: Check bereavement-related benefits
description: Check benefit entitlements: the Funeral Expenses Payment if a person on a qualifying benefit is paying for the funeral, and Bereavement Support Payment where there is a surviving spouse or civil partner.
